init.output:
Usage: hardkas init [options] [name]

Initialize a new HardKAS project [32mstable[39m

Arguments:
  name               Project name or directory

Options:
  --force            Overwrite existing hardkas.config.ts (in-place only)
                     (default: false)
  --template <type>  Project template for new projects (default: "basic")
  --network <name>   Default network for new projects (default: "simulated")
  --accounts <n>     Number of simulated accounts for new projects (default:
                     "3")
  --skip-install     Skip pnpm install for new projects (default: false)
  -h, --help         display help for command

doctor.output:
Usage: hardkas doctor [options] [module]

Perform a full system diagnostic and health report [32mstable[39m

Options:
  --json          Output results as stable JSON schema (default: false)
  --capabilities  Report local node capabilities (RPC, network, DAA) (default:
                  false)
  --consistency   Run advanced deterministic consistency checks (default:
                  false)
  --strict        Fail strictly (exit 1) if invariants or consistency checks
                  fail (default: false)
  -h, --help      display help for command

capabilities.output:
Usage: hardkas capabilities [options]

Show HardKAS capabilities and maturity level

Options:
  --json      Output as stable JSON schema (default: false)
  -h, --help  display help for command

accounts.output:
Usage: hardkas accounts [options] [command]

Manage HardKAS accounts

Options:
  -h, --help                       display help for command

Commands:
  list [options]                   List available HardKAS accounts
                                   [32mstable[39m
  real                             Persistent dev account store (L1)
  balance [options] <identifier>   Show account balance [32mstable[39m
  fund [options] <identifier>      Fund an account (Faucet)
  consolidate [options] <account>  Consolidate dust UTXOs into a single UTXO
                                   [37malpha[39m
  help [command]                   display help for command

localnet.output:
Usage: hardkas localnet [options] [command]

Manage localnet state and snapshots

Options:
  -h, --help                   display help for command

Commands:
  start [options]              Start localnet profile [37malpha[39m
  stop [options]               Stop localnet profile [37malpha[39m
  status [options]             Show localnet status [37malpha[39m
  fund [options] <identifier>  Fund a local Toccata/simnet account
                               [37malpha[39m
  account                      Manage simulated localnet accounts
  fork [options]               Fork state from a real Kaspa network for local
                               simulation [34mpreview[39m
  snapshot                     Manage HardKAS localnet snapshots
  help [command]               display help for command

tx.output:
Usage: hardkas tx [options] [command]

L1 Transaction commands

Options:
  -h, --help                          display help for command

Commands:
  profile [options] <path>            Show detailed mass and fee breakdown for
                                      a transaction plan [32mstable[39m
  batch [options]                     Process a batch of transactions
                                      sequentially [32mstable[39m
  plan [options]                      Build a transaction plan artifact
                                      [32mstable[39m
  sign [options] <planPath>           Sign a transaction plan artifact
                                      [32mstable[39m
  status [options] <path>             Show the signature coverage and status of
                                      a transaction artifact
  send [options] [signedPath]         Broadcast a signed transaction or send
                                      directly [32mstable[39m
  receipt [options] <txId>            Show transaction receipt [32mstable[39m
  wait [options] <txId>               Wait for transaction to be confirmed
                                      [32mstable[39m
  verify [options] <path>             Perform deep semantic verification of a
                                      transaction plan [34mpreview[39m
  trace <txId>                        Reconstruct the full operational trace of
                                      a transaction [35mresearch[39m
  compare <simulatedPath> <realPath>  Compare simulated vs real receipts for
                                      fidelity [32mstable[39m
  help [command]                      display help for command

artifacts.output:
Usage: hardkas [options] [command]

HardKAS: Kaspa-native developer operating environment

Options:
  -V, --version               output the version number
  -h, --help                  display help for command

Commands:
  init [options] [name]       Initialize a new HardKAS project [32mstable[39m
  up                          Boot or validate the HardKAS developer runtime
                              environment [32mstable[39m
  tx                          L1 Transaction commands
  artifact                    Manage HardKAS artifacts
  replay                      Manage HardKAS transaction replays
  rpc                         Kaspa RPC diagnostics and queries
  dag                         Simulate blockDAG operations (Localnet only)
  accounts                    Manage HardKAS accounts
  node                        Kaspa node management (Docker)
  config                      Manage HardKAS configuration
  query                       Query and introspect HardKAS artifacts, lineage,
                              and workflows
  test [options] [files...]   Run HardKAS tests against localnet
                              [32mstable[39m
  doctor [options] [module]   Perform a full system diagnostic and health
                              report [32mstable[39m
  verify [options]            Verify artifact integrity and lineage continuity
                              across the workspace [32mstable[39m
  verify-semantics [options]  Verify semantic truth agreement across all
                              HardKAS subsystems [37malpha[39m
  rebuild [options]           Reconstruct projections from committed canonical
                              artifacts [32mstable[39m
  run [options] <script>      Execute a TypeScript or JavaScript file with
                              HardKAS SDK injected [32mstable[39m
  lock                        Manage HardKAS workspace locks
  console [options]           Open an interactive REPL with HardKAS SDK
                              pre-loaded [32mstable[39m
  localnet                    Manage localnet state and snapshots
  deploy                      Track and manage deployments
  dev [options]               Local development and Igra-native environment
                              tools
  local                       Local development and environment lifecycle tools
  kaspa                       Kaspa L1 native developer tools
  dashboard                   Open the HardKAS Semantic Observability Dashboard
                              [37malpha[39m
  explain <id_or_path>        Provide a narrative causal explanation of a
                              deterministic artifact, transaction, or replay
                              [32mstable[39m
  torture                     HardKAS Semantic Torture Testing Suite
  telemetry                   Inspect, verify, and monitor the canonical
                              runtime pressure telemetry stream
  repair [options]            Attempt automatic recovery of corrupt projections
                              or append tails [37mbeta[39m
  rotate [options]            Rotate and archive telemetry streams
                              [37mbeta[39m
  inspect [options]           Inspect stream sizes and archive segments
                              [37mbeta[39m
  chaos [options]             Run the internal Chaos Engine to stress-test the
                              runtime [33mexperimental[39m
  status [options]            Display the current state of the local HardKAS
                              runtime workspace
  why [options] <artifactId>  Explain the causal lineage of a given artifact ID
  ci                          Continuous Integration and DevSecOps commands
  sandbox [options]           Start a temporary, ephemeral HardKAS local
                              experimentation environment
  silver                      SilverScript development tools (alpha)
  corpus                      Verify release fixture corpora
  zk                          Experimental local-only ZK proof artifact tools
                              (Outputs JSON by default)
  vprogs                      vProgs inspect-only builder surface (Outputs JSON
                              by default)
  programmability             Builder-ready local programmability surface
                              (Outputs JSON by default)
  dev-server                  Manage the local HardKAS dev server
                              [32mstable[39m
  security                    Security DX and safety verification tools
  workflow                    Programmable deterministic workflows and agent
                              orchestration [37malpha[39m
  help [command]              display help for command

artifact.output:
Usage: hardkas artifact [options] [command]

Manage HardKAS artifacts

Options:
  -h, --help                      display help for command

Commands:
  create [options] <type>         Create a new HardKAS artifact [37malpha[39m
  inspect [options] <id_or_path>  Deep inspect an artifact by ID or path
                                  [32mstable[39m
  verify [options] <path>         Verify an artifact's integrity and schema
                                  [32mstable[39m
  explain [options] <path>        Provide a human-readable operational summary
                                  of an artifact [32mstable[39m
  lineage [options] <path>        Show the provenance and operational history
                                  of an artifact [32mstable[39m
  help [command]                  display help for command

query.output:
Usage: hardkas query [options] [command]

Query and introspect HardKAS artifacts, lineage, and workflows

Options:
  -h, --help           display help for command

Commands:
  artifacts            Query artifact store [32mstable[39m
  store                Manage query store index [32mstable[39m
  lineage              Traverse artifact lineage [32mstable[39m
  replay               Inspect replay history and divergence [32mstable[39m
  dag                  Query simulated DAG state [35mresearch[39m
  events [options]     Query event log
  tx [options] <txId>  Aggregate all data for a transaction [32mstable[39m
  help [command]       display help for command

query store.output:
Usage: hardkas query store [options] [command]

Manage query store index [32mstable[39m

Options:
  -h, --help             display help for command

Commands:
  doctor [options]       Integrity and freshness check of the query store index
  migrate [options]      Apply pending schema migrations to the query store
  sync|index [options]   Synchronize the filesystem artifacts with the query
                         store index
  rebuild [options]      Force a complete rebuild of the query store index
  sql [options] <query>  Run a raw SQL query against the query store
  export [options]       Export logical store state to JSON
  help [command]         display help for command

security.output:
Usage: hardkas security [options] [command]

Security DX and safety verification tools

Options:
  -h, --help       display help for command

Commands:
  audit [options]  Audit workspace for DX safety and secret leakage
  help [command]   display help for command

programmability.output:
Usage: hardkas programmability [options] [command]

Builder-ready local programmability surface (Outputs JSON by default)

Options:
  -h, --help                display help for command

Commands:
  capabilities [options]    Show local programmability capabilities
  audit [options]           Audit the boundaries and claims of programmability
                            layers
  inspect [options] <path>  Inspect a Silver, ZK, or vProgs artifact
  verify [options] <path>   Verify a local programmability artifact
  corpus                    Verify local programmability corpora
  app                       Plan builder app surfaces
  help [command]            display help for command

zk.output:
Usage: hardkas zk [options] [command]

Experimental local-only ZK proof artifact tools (Outputs JSON by default)

Options:
  -h, --help              display help for command

Commands:
  capabilities [options]  Show experimental ZK lab capabilities
  proof                   Inspect and verify local proof artifacts
  corpus                  Verify experimental ZK fixture corpora
  help [command]          display help for command

vprogs.output:
Usage: hardkas vprogs [options] [command]

vProgs inspect-only builder surface (Outputs JSON by default)

Options:
  -h, --help                    display help for command

Commands:
  capabilities [options]        Show vProgs inspect capabilities
  status [options]              Show vProgs inspect status
  inspect [options] <artifact>  Inspect a local vProgs artifact without runtime
                                claims
  help [command]                display help for command

silver.output:
Usage: hardkas silver [options] [command]

SilverScript development tools (alpha)

Options:
  -h, --help                                display help for command

Commands:
  discovery                                 Experimental SilverScript discovery tools
  deploy-plan [options] <compile-artifact>  Plan a deployment of a compiled SilverScript on Kaspa
  deploy [options] <deploy-plan-artifact>   Execute a SilverScript deploy plan to create the UTXO on-chain
  spend-plan [options] <deploy-artifact>    Plan a spend of a deployed SilverScript UTXO
  spend <spend-plan-artifact>               Execute a SilverScript spend plan to spend the UTXO on-chain
  simulate                                  Local SilverScript/Toccata artifact lifecycle simulator
  doctor [options]                          Diagnose if the local environment can work with SilverScript
  compile [options] <file>                  Compile a SilverScript source file (.sil or .silver)
  inspect <artifact_path>                   Inspect a SilverScript compiled artifact or spend plan
  verify <artifact_path>                    Verify the deterministic hashes and metadata of a SilverScript artifact
  test [options] <file.sil>                 Run deterministic test harness for a SilverScript contract
  certify <file.sil>                        Execute the full certification pipeline for a SilverScript contract
  help [command]                            display help for command

l2.output:
Usage: hardkas l2 [options] [command]

Layer 2 (Igra) management

Options:
  -h, --help                   display help for command

Commands:
  networks [options]           List available L2 network profiles
  profile                      L2 profile management
  tx                           Igra transaction management
  contract                     Igra contract management
  bridge                       Igra bridge awareness
  rpc                          Igra RPC diagnostics
  balance [options] <address>  Check Igra L2 balance
  nonce [options] <address>    Check Igra L2 nonce
  help [command]               display help for command

test.output:
Usage: hardkas test [options] [files...]

Run HardKAS tests against localnet [32mstable[39m

Options:
  --network <network>      Network to test against (default: "simnet")
  --watch                  Watch for changes (default: false)
  --json                   Output results as JSON (default: false)
  --mass-report            Show mass/fee report after test run (default: false)
  --mass-snapshot <label>  Save mass snapshot for regression detection
  --mass-compare <label>   Compare against saved mass snapshot
  -h, --help               display help for command
